summary: Iteration 6 bug-fix testing complete. All 3 Interview.jsx fixes verified. Backend regression: 27/27 tests PASS (24 prior iter4+iter5 + 3 new iter6 null-body-language tests). Frontend: BUG 2 (video component remount on question nav) fixed — iv-video is SAME DOM node across Next and Prev. BUG 3 (textarea value lost across nav) fixed — 'my typed answer preserved across navigation' retained after Next→Prev. BUG 1 (camera leak on analyzer init failure) verified via static code review — initCamera lines 22-59 correctly implements split try/catch with track cleanup, streamRef nulling, and srcObject reset on analyzer failure; guard uses `streamRef.current && ready` to allow partial-init retry. Zero console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Interview.jsx initCamera (lines 22-59): split-try-block pattern is CORRECT — step 1 handles getUserMedia failure with 'permission denied', step 2 handles analyzer failure with cleanup (stop tracks, null streamRef, clear srcObject) and shows the correct 'Body-language models failed to load' message. Guard `if (streamRef.current && ready)` enables retry after partial init failure. Well done.

Interview.jsx VideoAnswer (line 290): `key={current}` removed, `questionIndex={current}` added. Component now persists across question navigation — camera+analyzer are initialized once. Confirmed via DOM identity check.

Interview.jsx handleAnswerComplete (lines 212-230) and textarea onChange (line 306): both correctly use functional setState (prev => ...) so late transcriptions and concurrent edits do not overwrite each other. Also `question_index` is captured at record-start in VideoAnswer.start (line 91: `capturedIdx = questionIndex`) and threaded through onComplete, so late transcripts land on the correct slot even after user navigates.
Iter5 carry-over observations still stand (build_body_language_summary >0 filter drops genuine 0 values; wpm synthesis when durations missing). Not blocking, not touched this iter.

Minor: cleanup useEffect (line 78-81) runs only on unmount — good. But the live-meter RAF loop (line 62-75) references analyzerRef.current which may be null before init; that's guarded (`if (analyzerRef.current?.live)`) — safe.
test_credentials: Fresh signups: backend qa+iter6_<ts>@talentiq.dev / Password123!; frontend qa+ui_iter6_<ts>_<rand>@talentiq.dev / Password123!. JWT localStorage key: tiq_token.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 backend test users (iter6) with 3 interviews each covering mixed-null, all-null, all-zero body_language submits.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite now has 3 files: test_linkedin_and_interview.py (iter4), test_iter5_refactor_regression.py (iter5), test_iter6_null_body_language.py (iter6). Together cover auth+profile+linkedin+interview_start+interview_submit (with/without/mixed-null body_language) + trends endpoint. For UI camera-dependent flows, headless browsers cannot grant camera access — verify via DOM identity checks (window.__vid === querySelector) for remount detection, and API-level integration via signup+localStorage token injection. Interview.jsx VideoAnswer key was removed intentionally in iter6, so remount checks should be part of any future interview-page regression.
rca of the issue: N/A — Iteration 6 was itself a bug-fix iteration; testing confirmed all 3 fixes work correctly. No new bugs found.
